changes_summary: Addressed 4 significant flags: (1) Fixed payload placement — scalars go in top-level segment params, not inside orchestratorDetails, matching how ctx.segment_params works in the worker. (2) Added retry fallback for frontend resolver — when resolveSegmentLayout returns undefined for non-zero segments with missing siblings, preserve scalar fields from origParams (previous task attempt). (3) Clarified timeline semantics — documented that extract_segment_structure_guidance uses GUIDANCE timeline (not stitched), which is existing behavior and correct for the standalone path; the plan preserves this split. (4) Dropped Step 5 entirely (array removal) — segment_frames_expanded and frame_overlap_expanded are consumed by 6+ systems (chaining, completion normalization, segment data transforms, shot editor, image service, diagnostics) and must remain in the payload. Added backwards-compatible fallback paths in Steps 3-4 so the worker gracefully handles in-flight tasks that lack the new scalar fields.
questions: For the frontend resolver: when resolveSegmentLayout returns undefined for a non-zero segment AND origParams has no previous scalar fields (brand-new segment with no prior attempts and no queryable siblings), the worker will fall back to array-based computation. Is this acceptable, or should we add a harder error?

success_criteria.criterion: Worker guide_builder.py prefers stitched_start_frame/guidance_start_frame scalars from segment_params over recomputing from arrays
Worker guide_builder.py falls back to array-based computation when scalar fields are absent (backwards compatibility)
Orchestrator segment payloads include stitched_start_frame and guidance_start_frame as top-level scalar fields (not inside orchestrator_details)
Frontend individualTravelSegment resolver computes and includes scalar start frame fields in top-level params
Frontend resolver preserves scalar fields from origParams on retries when resolveSegmentLayout returns undefined
extract_segment_structure_guidance accepts optional pre-computed position parameters alongside existing array parameters
All existing worker tests pass (uv run pytest)
TypeScript compilation passes for the create-task edge function
segment_frames_expanded and frame_overlap_expanded arrays remain in payload (not removed)
Travel generation with 3+ segments and travel guidance produces correct guidance positioning (manual smoke test)

assumptions: Both stitched_start_frame and guidance_start_frame are needed because two distinct timelines are actively used (travel guidance vs structure guidance)

segment_frames_expanded and frame_overlap_expanded arrays must remain in the payload indefinitely — they serve chaining, completion, app UI, and diagnostics consumers that are out of scope for this refactoring
The calculateStitchedStart logic in TypeScript can be a simple inline function mirroring calculate_segment_stitched_position from segments.py
Worker-created segments (pass-through path in individualTravelSegment.ts) will get the scalar fields from the orchestrator, so no change needed in the pass-through branch
Segment 0 always has stitched_start_frame=0 and guidance_start_frame=0, so the default of 0 via missing field is correct
The origParams spread in the frontend resolver preserves fields from previous task attempts, making scalar fields available on retries even when sibling queries fail
extract_segment_structure_guidance correctly uses the GUIDANCE timeline for standalone segments — this is existing behavior, not a bug to fix in this refactoring
